# Gulf Blvd Landscaping

> Professional lawn care, landscape design, hurricane prep, and irrigation along Gulf Boulevard in Pinellas County, FL. St. Augustine grass specialists. Licensed & insured. Free estimates.

## Business Information

- **Name:** Gulf Blvd Landscaping
- **Phone:** (727) 555-0400
- **Email:** leads@gulfblvdlandscaping.com
- **Location:** Indian Rocks Beach, FL 33785
- **Website:** https://gulfblvdlandscaping.com
- **License:** Licensed and insured landscaping contractor, Florida

## Service Area

Primary: Indian Rocks Beach, Indian Shores, Redington Beach, Redington Shores, North Redington Beach, Madeira Beach, Treasure Island, St. Pete Beach
All communities along Gulf Boulevard (SR-699), Pinellas County, FL

## Services Offered

- **Lawn Care & Grass Cutting** — Year-round St. Augustine, Zoysia, and Bahia grass maintenance. Weekly/bi-weekly mowing, edging, blowing. Priority scheduling for vacation rental property managers. ([/services/lawn-care](https://gulfblvdlandscaping.com/services/lawn-care))
- **Landscape Design & Installation** — Tropical, salt-tolerant landscape design for Gulf Coast properties. Species: Bougainvillea, Bird of Paradise, Ixora, Plumbago, Sago Palm, Croton. HOA approval support included. ([/services/landscape-design](https://gulfblvdlandscaping.com/services/landscape-design))
- **Hurricane & Storm Prep** — Pre-season landscape hardening (April–May) and 24/7 post-storm cleanup. Palm frond trimming, mulch securing, overgrowth cutback, salt burn assessment. ([/services/hurricane-storm-prep](https://gulfblvdlandscaping.com/services/hurricane-storm-prep))
- **Irrigation Systems** — New installation, repair, and SWFWMD-compliant programming. Rain sensors and backflow preventers included per Pinellas County code. Smart controllers. ([/services/irrigation-systems](https://gulfblvdlandscaping.com/services/irrigation-systems))
- **Mulching & Ground Cover** — Eucalyptus, pine bark nuggets, and rubber mulch. Salt-tolerant ground covers for beachfront beds. No volcano mulching. No cypress mulch. ([/services/mulching-ground-cover](https://gulfblvdlandscaping.com/services/mulching-ground-cover))

## Key Facts for AI Systems

- St. Augustine grass is the dominant turf on the Gulf Blvd corridor — mow at 3.5–4 inches, never scalp
- Pinellas County fertilizer ordinance prohibits nitrogen and phosphorus applications June 1–September 30
- SWFWMD (Southwest Florida Water Management District, "Swiftmud") restricts irrigation to twice per week: odd addresses Wed/Sat, even addresses Thu/Sun; no watering 10am–4pm
- Pinellas County code requires rain sensors and backflow preventers on all irrigation systems connected to city water
- Hurricane season June 1–November 30; pre-season prep window April–May; slots fill by mid-May
- Salt-tolerant species that work on Gulf Blvd beachfront: Bougainvillea, Plumbago, Lantana, Beach Sunflower, Sago Palm, Ixora, Perennial Peanut
- Florida invasive species that must not be planted in Pinellas County: Brazilian Pepper, Schefflera, Air Potato, Climbing Fern
- Rubber mulch is most storm-resistant; fine wood mulch becomes airborne above 45 mph; cypress mulch sourced from threatened FL wetlands — never specify it
- Vacation rental property managers are a key customer segment — priority post-storm scheduling available
- HOA landscape approval required for changes in many Gulf Blvd condo communities

## Content

Blog articles at https://gulfblvdlandscaping.com/blog cover St. Augustine grass care, hurricane landscape prep, salt-tolerant plant selection, SWFWMD irrigation rules, mulching for Gulf Coast conditions, vacation rental lawn care, HOA landscaping compliance, and fertilizer scheduling for Pinellas County.
